questions.question: In digital circuit design, what does the 'fan-out' of a logic gate primarily refer to?
options: The maximum number of input signals it can accept.
The number of output signals it generates.
The maximum number of similar gates that can be driven by its output while maintaining valid logic levels.
The amount of heat dissipated by the gate.
The speed at which the gate can switch its output state.

questions.question: In the physical design phase of an Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) or System-on-Chip (SoC), what is the primary objective of the 'Place and Route' stage?
options: To convert the Register Transfer Level (RTL) description into a gate-level netlist.
To verify the logical equivalence between the RTL and gate-level netlists.
To determine the physical location of logic cells (placement) and connect them with wires (routing) on the silicon die.
To analyze the timing performance of the circuit and ensure it meets specifications.
To generate test patterns for manufacturing defect detection.

questions.question: In a processor's cache memory system, which cache replacement policy typically evicts the block that has not been accessed for the longest period of time?
options: First-In, First-Out (FIFO)
Least Recently Used (LRU)
Random Replacement (RR)
Most Recently Used (MRU)
Optimal Replacement (OPT)

questions.question: When designing a System-on-Chip (SoC) where different functional blocks operate with independent and unsynchronized clock signals, what is the primary challenge addressed by Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) synchronization techniques?
options: Minimizing static power consumption in the inactive blocks.
Ensuring data integrity and preventing metastability when signals transition between clock domains.
Optimizing the physical placement of clock buffers to reduce clock skew.
Converting analog signals into digital format for processing.
Detecting and correcting single-event upsets (SEUs) caused by radiation.

questions.question: In digital circuits, the 'propagation delay' (tpd) of a logic gate primarily quantifies which characteristic?
options: The maximum number of inputs the gate can accept.
The time required for the gate's output to respond to a change in its input(s).
The total power consumed by the gate during operation.
The physical area occupied by the gate on the silicon die.
The frequency at which the gate can reliably operate.
